data.title: Nike partners with Kool-Aid for the first time with a Ja Morant basketball shoe
data.description:
Nike (NYSE:NKE) adding to its long history of deals with NBA stars with a new collaboration with Ja Morant and Kraft-Heinz's (NASDAQ:KHC) Kool-Aid brand. 

The new Nike x Kool-Aid Ja 2 was highlighted as being inspired by Memphis Grizzlies star Ja Morant's childhood memories and Kool-Aid's iconic flavors. Nike (NYSE:NKE) said the partnership merges sneaker culture, nostalgia, and creativity, reflecting Ja’s personality both on and off the court. The shoe design draws from Ja’s favorite Kool-Aid flavors, such as Blue Raspberry and Cherry, blending them into bold gradients alongside playful details like the Kool-Aid Man’s “Ohhh Yeah!” catchphrase and glow-in-the-dark outsoles. 

The collaboration also celebrates fans’ creativity in mixing flavors, which is a trend popularized on TikTok. 

The collection will feature an initial drop in May with performance-focused designs, and a second in July highlighting Kool-Aid's signature red colorway. To mark the launch, Kool-Aid is introducing a limited-edition flavor pack for fans to mix and match iconic flavors. Select fans can win custom Kool-Aid dubraes and laces to personalize their sneakers. 

Nike (NKE) has a long history of iconic shoe deals with NBA stars, including the iconic Michael Jordan Air Jordan line that revolutionized sneaker culture. Currently, LeBron James' signature shoes, Kevin Durant's KD series, and Giannis Antetokounmpo’s Zoom Freak line are all seeing strong sales trends.

Shares of Nike (NKE) were up 1.5% in premarket trading on Monday, amid a day of early gains for the stock market and companies tied to China manufacturing. Shares of Nike (NKE) are down 28.1% on a year-to-date basis.

data.title: BP makes new oil discovery off Louisiana coast
data.description:
BP (NYSE:BP) +3.1% pre-market Monday after saying it discovered oil at the U.S. Far South prospect in the Gulf of Mexico, with preliminary data supporting a potentially commercial volume of hydrocarbons.

BP (NYSE:BP) said it drilled the exploration well in Green Canyon Block 584 in more than 4K ft of water ~120 miles off the Louisiana coast, and was drilled to a total depth of 23,830 ft.

The Far South discovery is is owned by operator BP (BP) with a 57.5% stake and Chevron (CVX) with a 42.5% holding, and is located four miles north of the Constellation field.

The company said the Far South discovery demonstrates that the U.S. Gulf remains "an area of incredible growth and opportunity for BP."

BP (BP) said it plans to raise its production in the U.G. Gulf to 400K boe/day by 2030, and expects to boost global upstream production to 2.3M-2.5M boe/day by 2030, with the capacity to increase production out to 2035.

data.title: Signing Day Sports jumps 43%, seeks to buy blockchAIn Digital Infrastructure
Crocs warns on the negative impact of tariffs on its business
data.description:
Crocs (NASDAQ:CROX) warned its employees to closely manage expenses due to the higher supply chain expenses the company anticipates due to the volatility around the trade war between the U.S. and other nations. Crocs (NASDAQ:CROX) produces about half of its products in Vietnam, which is a target for higher tariff rates. 

In a company memo spotted by Bloomberg by Chief Executive Officer Andrew Rees, Crocs (CROX) staff were told to remain prudent for the remainder of the year and be thoughtful about travel and which initiatives to prioritize. In addition, the Crocs (CROX) board of directors' compensation committee has agreed to establish more realistic targets for the footwear maker's internal incentives plan as a result of the economic environment.

"Given the state of the escalating and uncertain tariff environment, we are focused on managing our business prudently to maximize shareholder value over the medium to long-term," updated a Crocs (CROX) spokesperson.

Shares of Crocs (CROX) edged 1.0% higher in premarket trading. The stock is down 15.5% on a year-to-date basis. Short interest on CROX stands at 14.3% of the total float.

data.title: Enlight raises $1.5B in project finance
data.description:
Enlight Renewable Energy (NASDAQ:ENLT) announced the financial close for project Quail Ranch located near Albuquerque, New Mexico, USA.

The company, through its U.S. subsidiary Clenera secured $243M in construction financing commitments for the project.

Combining 128 MW solar generation with 400 MWh of battery storage capacity, Quail Ranch is scheduled for completion towards the end of 2025.

The three projects have a combined capacity of 1.4 FGW and are expected to generate annual revenues of $135M-$140M and EBITDA of $100M-$110M when commencing operations in 2025-2026.

“Additionally, Enlight is focused on advancing the development of two additional megaprojects in the western U.S. with a combined capacity of 2.6 FGW, and which are located in areas with some of the highest solar irradiation in the country. The new projects are part of the Company's third wave in the U.S., and construction is expected to begin in the coming months.” said Gilad Yavetz, CEO.

data.title: British Steel: UK minister says he wouldn't bring China into 'sensitive' steel industry again
data.description:
The UK government took effective control of Britain’s last remaining factory that makes steel from scratch from its Chinese owners, after lawmakers approved an emergency rescue.

The UK was "naive" to allow its sensitive steel industry to fall into the hands of a Chinese company, Britain's business secretary said on Sunday after the government took control of British Steel. 

Reynolds said the refusal of China’s Jingye Group to accept a roughly 500 million pound ($654 million) government aid package last week to stop irrevocable damage to blast furnaces left the government with no alternative but to intervene directly.

Blast furnaces are difficult to restart once switched off, and failure to secure enough supplies to keep them running could seriously damage the plant.

"If we hadn't acted, the blast furnaces were gone and in the UK primary steel production would have gone," Reynolds said on Sunday.

The site in northern England had faced imminent closure, and Prime Minister Keir Starmer said urgent action was needed to prevent its blast furnaces from going out.

China ETFs: (KWEB), (PGJ), (FXI), (ASHR), (YIN), (YANG), (GXC), (FXP), (KBA), (CXSE), (CNYA), (CHAU), (FLCH), (YXI), (FCA), (KALL), (JCHI)

data.title: Parsons wins seat on $1.5B Air Force Civil Engineering Center contract
data.description:
Parsons (NYSE:PSN) has won a seat on a $1.5B indefinite-delivery, indefinite-quantity multiple award task order contract by the Air Force Civil Engineering Center to provide architect-engineer services addressing environmental issues.

Under this contract, Parsons will compete for task orders based on qualifications to address environmental issues, including restoration and compliance; conservation and planning and programming; emerging issues and contaminants; and quality and other areas of essential support, at United States Air Force installations worldwide and for other federal clients such as the Defense Logistics Agency and NASA.

The contract includes a five-year base period and five one-year option periods, including an option to extend another six months.

Source: Press Release

data.title: Tesla faces an uphill climb to match last year's Q2 deliveries tally
data.description:
Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) delivered 443,956 vehicles in Q2 of 2024, which is a mark that the company may struggle to match in Q2 of this year. Following the company's weak Q1 deliveries report, analysts and prediction markets now expect deliveries to range between 350,000 and 375,000 vehicles, reflecting a potential year-over-year decline once again.

The drop is attributed partly to the Model Y's production changeover, but other issues persist. Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) ended the quarter with record high inventory levels and no backlog for the new Model Y, which is readily available in many U.S. regions. Of course, that scenario contrasts with prior years, when Tesla's (TSLA) demand often exceeded supply.

While Tesla (TSLA) remains the U.S. electric vehicle leader, the company's dominant market share continues to slip amid competition from brands such as Rivian Automotive (RIVN), Lucid Group (LCID), Ford Motor (F), General Motors (GM), Honda (HMC), Hyundai (OTCPK:HYMTF) and Ford Motor (F). Analysts think the decline reflects broader challenges for Tesla (TSLA), including public backlash against CEO Elon Musk and the intensifying competition in the EV market. The upshot is that Tesla (TSLA) could struggle to match last year's deliveries tally unless there is a strong response to new model introductions (Model 2 or Model Q) and the refreshed Model Y.

data.title: European, US chip stocks gain amid temporary reprieve from Trump's tariffs
data.description:
Several European and U.S. chip stocks were in the green premarket on Monday after the Trump administration temporarily exempted electronics such as smartphones and computers, among other items, mostly imported from China, from reciprocal tariffs.

AI chipmakers Nvidia (NVDA) and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) both jumped around 2%, each. In addition, Broadcom (AVGO) and Qualcomm (QCOM) climbed about 1%, each.

The exclusions were published on Friday by the U.S. Customs and Border Protection agency and provided a big relief to American tech giants such as Apple (AAPL), Nvidia (NVDA) and Dell Technologies  (DELL). However, Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick said that it was a temporary reprieve and added that, "all those products are going to come under semiconductors, and they're going to have a special focus type of tariff to make sure that those products get reshored."

European chip stocks such as Dutch semiconductor equipment maker ASML (ASML) was up about 1% while French-Italian STMicroelectronics (STM) was largely flat, but in the red, premarket on Monday. Shares of Netherlands-based NXP Semiconductors' (NXPI) rose about 2%, while Neubiberg-based Infineon Technologies (OTCQX:IFNNY) (OTCQX:IFNNF) jumped about 3% in Germany.

On Friday, it was reported that China's import tariffs on U.S. goods provide an exemption for U.S. semiconductor companies that outsource their manufacturing. Reportedly, U.S. companies such as Qualcomm (QCOM) and AMD, which outsource manufacturing to Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM), are exempted as China's regulators classify these chips as originating from Taiwan.

Intel (INTC) and Micron (MU) surged around 4%, each premarket on Monday. GlobalFoundries (GFS) and Lattice Semiconductor (LSCC) and Marvell Technology (MRVL) each grew nearly 2%. Texas Instruments (TXN) rose about 1%, while Analog Devices (ADI) was largely flat, but in the green. Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) was largely flat but in the red.

Meanwhile, chip equipment makers: Applied Materials (AMAT) and KLA (KLAC) each rose nearly 1%. Lam Research (LRCX) was up roughly 0.5%.
